_comment: Canned classifier output keyed by surviving item id (ft-002 dedup'd, ft-014 dropped by look-ahead). ft-010 is materiality 1 and is dropped by the materiality floor. Categories drawn from the fintech taxonomy: Product, Partnership, Regulatory, Financial.
tldr: Affirm gaps up ~11% on an exclusive Walmart OnePay BNPL deal; Upstart pops on an above-guidance preannounce while Shift4 slips on a $1.5B European acquisition.

ft-001.summary: Affirm signed an exclusive multiyear deal to power BNPL installment loans in Walmart's OnePay app across U.S. checkout, replacing the prior provider, with GMV contribution starting next quarter.

ft-003.summary: Upstart preannounced Q2 revenue of $228-232M, above its $213M guidance, and reinstated full-year guidance on stronger funding-partner demand.

ft-004.summary: A federal court vacated the CFPB rule applying credit-card dispute and chargeback rules to BNPL lenders, easing near-term compliance though an appeal is possible.

ft-005.summary: SoFi topped 11 million members with record direct-deposit adoption, a key deposit-funding driver, and reiterated full-year EPS guidance.

ft-006.summary: Block began a national rollout of Cash App Borrow after its bank partner cleared regulators, a key lever for Cash App monetization.

ft-007.summary: PayPal struck a deal to add its branded checkout button across a major commerce platform's merchants, supporting transaction-margin-dollar growth.

ft-008.summary: Robinhood launched tokenized U.S. equities for EU customers on a layer-2 chain, broadening its crypto and international lineup.

ft-009.summary: Nubank received a full banking license in Mexico, unlocking payroll accounts and a broader deposit and lending suite in its second-largest market.

ft-010.summary: Toast will webcast a fireside chat at an upcoming investor conference.

ft-011.summary: BILL Holdings reported record Spend & Expense card volume, lifting interchange-driven revenue on small-business adoption.

ft-012.summary: Shift4 agreed to acquire a European payments processor for about $1.5B to expand international acquiring, expecting EBITDA accretion within a year.

ft-013.summary: Marqeta renewed its core card-issuing program with Block through 2030, removing a major contract-concentration overhang on revised economics.

ft-015.summary: Subprime consumer delinquencies rose a third straight month, which analysts warn could lift funding costs and loss provisions for consumer lenders.

ft-016.summary: Affirm priced a $750M asset-backed securitization at tighter spreads than its prior deal, signaling improved funding conditions ahead of the holiday season.
